Display.Title: Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior
Display.Observations: Ceremonial upholstered seating preserved within the Napoleon III Apartments, a largely intact Second Empire interior created in 1861 for Napoleon III’s Minister of State. The chairs organize the room through repetition, ornament, and controlled spacing, producing a social field oriented toward reception rather than relaxation. In the museum setting, their original function survives as image and atmosphere while bodily use is suspended.
Display.Caption: Photographic record of ceremonial salon seating in the Napoleon III Apartments, Louvre Museum.
Display.Map_Label: SS-018 — Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior
Display.Header_Subtitle: SS-018 — Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior

Display.Title: Molded Plywood Lounge Chair — Sidewalk Resale Display
Display.Materials: molded plywood, vinyl upholstery, metal base
Display.Observations: A molded plywood lounge chair positioned within a sidewalk resale display. Though structurally capable of supporting extended rest, its placement among stacked and adjacent furniture withdraws it from immediate use. The chair operates as a commodity image rather than a seating instrument, inviting visual evaluation instead of bodily occupation. Comfort is implied but suspended pending purchase.
Display.Caption: Photographic record of molded plywood seating displayed for resale.
Display.Map_Label: SS-016 — Molded Plywood Lounge Chair — Sidewalk Resale Display
Display.Header_Subtitle: SS-016 — Molded Plywood Lounge Chair — Sidewalk Resale Display

Display.Title: Bentwood Armchair — Retail Seating
Display.Observations: A bentwood armchair positioned within a retail environment, offering brief support without encouraging extended occupation. The lightweight frame and cane surfaces suggest permeability and airflow, reinforcing short-duration use. The form accommodates pause during browsing rather than sustained rest, balancing invitation with subtle temporal limitation.
Display.Caption: Photographic record of seating that invites pause without encouraging duration.

Display.Title: Lounge Chair — Exhibition Proxy for Rest
Display.Materials: molded plastic, metal frame, wood armrest
Display.Observations: Presented as seating but withdrawn from bodily use, the chair operates as a visual proxy for rest. Its form suggests comfort while denying occupation, integrating into the exhibition’s viewing apparatus. The object becomes something to be looked at rather than inhabited.
Display.Caption: Photographic record of seating presented for viewing rather than use.
Display.Map_Label: SS-014 — Lounge Chair — Exhibition Proxy for Rest
Display.Header_Subtitle: SS-014 — Lounge Chair — Exhibition Proxy for Rest

Display.Title: Ornamental Armchairs — Storefront Threshold
Display.Materials: carved wood, gilded detailing, upholstered fabric
Display.Observations: Two ornate chairs frame the threshold between street and interior. Elevated scale and decorative excess suggest ceremonial authority rather than rest. Although positioned in public space, the chairs do not invite occupation. Seating operates symbolically—signaling luxury and hierarchy—while regulating who may cross the threshold and under what posture.
Display.Caption: Photographic record of ornamental seating used as storefront display.

Display.Title: Modified Armchair — Exhibition Installation
Display.Observations: A modified armchair is presented within an exhibition setting, where the addition of human hair destabilizes the boundary between furniture, body, and sculptural object. The familiar language of seating remains legible, but comfort is interrupted by a material treatment that suggests grooming, accumulation, and intimate residue. Rather than supporting the body directly, the chair stages sitting as a withheld possibility and redirects attention toward display, performance, and ritualized care.
Display.Caption: Photographic record of modified seating incorporated into an exhibition installation.

Display.Title: Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior
Display.Materials: upholstered fabric, gilded detailing, carved wood
Display.Observations: Ceremonial upholstered seating preserved within the Napoleon III Apartments at the Louvre, a Second Empire interior created in 1861 for Napoleon III’s Minister of State. Designed for elite reception rather than informal rest, the chair participates in a larger decorative system in which gilding, upholstery, and controlled spacing organize visibility and social order. In the museum setting, its original function survives as atmosphere and historical display while bodily use is suspended.
Display.Caption: Photographic record of ceremonial upholstered seating within the Louvre’s Napoleon III Apartments.
Display.Map_Label: SS-004 — Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior
Display.Header_Subtitle: SS-004 — Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior

Display.Title: Decorative Side Chair, Outdoor Market
Display.Observations: The chair’s needlepoint-style upholstered seat shifts emphasis from comfort to visual narrative. Before it is encountered as seating, it presents itself as decorated object. In the market setting, domestic ornament becomes commodity, and the act of sitting is momentarily deferred by the demand to look.
Display.Caption: Photographic record of side chair with figural needlepoint-style upholstery in market setting.
Display.Map_Label: SS-003 — Decorative Side Chair, Outdoor Market
Display.Header_Subtitle: SS-003 — Decorative Side Chair, Outdoor Market

Resting.Title: Deep-Seated Armchair — Domestic Rest
Resting.Observations: A deep-seated upholstered armchair positioned within a private interior and shown in active use. Its form supports extended rest and asymmetrical bodily orientation, allowing the occupant to recline and elevate the legs without maintaining formal posture. Unlike display or transitional seating, the chair accommodates duration, privacy, and release, functioning as a domestic structure for sustained comfort rather than regulation.
Resting.Caption: Photographic record of deep-seated armchair in active domestic use.

Resting.Title: Lakeside Armchairs — Horizon-Oriented Rest
Resting.Observations: Seating oriented toward a fixed horizon encourages sustained stillness. The body is permitted to remain without instruction, endpoint, or display.
Resting.Caption: Photographic record of lakeside balcony seating.

Resting.Title: Reclining Loungers — Hospitality Lawn
Resting.Observations: A continuous row of reclining loungers arranged in parallel, facing a shared view. Spacing and orientation are uniform. Although designed for rest, the seating prescribes how leisure is performed—alignment, duration, and relational positioning are structured. Comfort is abundant but organized.
Resting.Caption: Photographic record of leisure seating aligned for guest use.

Resting.Title: Child-Sized Chair, Private Residence
Resting.Observations: This child-sized side chair reproduces the formal language of adult domestic furniture in miniature. Its carved crest rail, upright back, and upholstered check seat soften the object’s disciplinary function by lending it familiarity. Scale operates as the primary regulatory device, training posture and placement through proportion and introducing the child to domestic codes of order, restraint, and presentation.
Resting.Caption: Photographic record of child-sized side chair in domestic interior.

Waiting.Title: Dining Chairs (Matched Set) — Domestic Displacement
Waiting.Observations: A matched set of dining chairs positioned outdoors after removal from their intended interior setting. Designed for shared domestic gathering, they remain oriented toward collective use while temporarily suspending it. The arrangement preserves social possibility, but shifts it into a provisional condition between abandonment, pickup, and reassignment.
Waiting.Caption: Photographic record of dining chairs displaced from domestic interior to curbside exterior.
Waiting.Map_Label: SS-013 — Dining Chairs (Matched Set) — Domestic Displacement
Waiting.Header_Subtitle: SS-013 — Dining Chairs (Matched Set) — Domestic Displacement

Waiting.Title: Upholstered Armchair — Roadside (Winter)
Waiting.Observations: Domestic upholstered seating is displaced into unmanaged public transit space. Environmental conditions temporarily suspend use, rendering comfort hypothetical rather than accessible. The object remains structurally intact but environmentally compromised.
Waiting.Caption: Photographic record of upholstered seating observed roadside during winter.

Waiting.Title: Painted Ladder-Back Chair — Commercial Interior
Waiting.Observations: A small red chair is positioned within a retail environment, where its cracked painted finish and worn surface foreground prolonged use and material aging. The object remains legible as functional seating, yet its visual presence exceeds its practical scale. Within the shop, it contributes to an atmosphere shaped by reuse, accumulation, and the display of domestic objects as both utility and character.
Waiting.Caption: Photographic record of painted wooden seating observed in a commercial interior.
Waiting.Map_Label: SS-006 — Painted Ladder-Back Chair — Commercial Interior
Waiting.Header_Subtitle: SS-006 — Painted Ladder-Back Chair — Commercial Interior

Waiting.Title: Informal Armchair Arrangement, Sidewalk
Waiting.Observations: A pair of upholstered armchairs and a small table are positioned on a rug directly within the sidewalk, forming a temporary domestic zone in public space. The grouping imports the visual and social language of the living room into the street, offering comfort, pause, and conversation where movement would normally dominate. Its placement destabilizes the boundary between private interiority and public thoroughfare, making domestic space briefly visible as an improvised urban condition.
Waiting.Caption: Photographic record of improvised domestic seating arrangement on sidewalk.

Working.Title: Student Desk-Chair — Classroom Synchronization
Working.Observations: The desk-chair integrates seating and work surface into a single unit, limiting bodily repositioning. Orientation is fixed toward the front of the room, aligning attention collectively. Scale and rigidity restrict lateral movement; comfort is secondary to endurance. Seating functions as a synchronizing device, holding multiple bodies in parallel states of instructional readiness.
Working.Caption: Photographic record of classroom seating during instructional time.

Working.Title: Artist’s Step Chair — Improvised Seating
Working.Observations: Designed to support standing reach rather than seated rest, the object is pressed into improvised sitting. The body compensates for inadequate scale and support, remaining active rather than settled. Duration is structurally limited.
Working.Caption: Photographic record of artist’s step chair used as improvised seating.
Working.Map_Label: SS-008 — Artist’s Step Chair — Improvised Seating
Working.Header_Subtitle: SS-008 — Artist’s Step Chair — Improvised Seating
